openmeter/productcatalog/addon/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing addon.Repository: CRUD + list for add-ons and their rate cards. All operations wrap adapter methods with entutils.TransactingRepo so the ctx-bound transaction is always honored.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping
patterns.description: Every exported adapter method wraps its body in entutils.TransactingRepo[T, *adapter](ctx, a, fn) to rebind to any caller-supplied Ent transaction carried in ctx.

patterns.name: Tx / WithTx / Self trio
patterns.description: adapter implements transaction.Driver via Tx() (HijackTx), WithTx() (returns new *adapter bound to txClient), and Self() — required by entutils.TransactingRepo.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txClient := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txClient.Client(), ...} }

patterns.name: Config + Validate constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates all required fields (Client, Logger) before constructing. Config implements models.Validator.

patterns.name: Eager loading via WithRatecards
patterns.description: All queries that return an Addon call query.WithRatecards(AddonEagerLoadRateCardsFn) so rate cards are always populated; PlanAddons are loaded only when Expand.PlanAddons is true.

patterns.name: mapping.go: FromAddonRow converts Ent row to domain type
patterns.description: All type conversions live in mapping.go (FromAddonRow, FromAddonRateCardRow, FromPlanAddonRow, asAddonRateCardRow). Do not embed conversion logic inside CRUD methods.

patterns.name: Soft delete via DeletedAt
patterns.description: DeleteAddon sets DeletedAt to time.Now().UTC() via UpdateOneID; ListAddons excludes deleted rows with addondb.DeletedAtIsNil() unless IncludeDeleted is set.

patterns.name: Status filtering via time predicates
patterns.description: AddonStatus is derived from EffectiveFrom/EffectiveTo columns at query time: active = EffectiveFrom<=now AND (EffectiveTo>=now OR nil), draft = both nil, archived = EffectiveTo<now.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, New constructor, adapter struct, Tx/WithTx/Self transaction plumbing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never remove Tx/WithTx/Self — they are required by entutils.TransactingRepo. Always validate Config before constructing.

key_file_guides.role: All CRUD + list methods for add-ons and their rate cards. Contains rateCardBulkCreate helper and addonEagerLoadActivePlans closure.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every method must wrap its fn body with TransactingRepo. UpdateAddon replaces all rate cards by delete+bulk-create; partial updates on individual cards are not supported.

key_file_guides.role: Bidirectional mapping between Ent rows and domain types: FromAddonRow, FromAddonRateCardRow, FromPlanAddonRow, asAddonRateCardRow, plus helpers that reuse plan/phase/ratecard converters from the plan adapter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TaxConfig.BackfillTaxConfig must be called after mapping TaxCode edge to ensure legacy field compatibility. BillingCadence uses ISOString parse/stringify, never raw string.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests against a real Postgres DB using pctestutils.NewTestEnv. Tests cover create/get/list/update/delete and status-filter logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use context.WithCancel — prefer t.Context() for new tests. TestEnv is closed in t.Cleanup.

anti_patterns: Accepting *entdb.Client directly in helper functions without wrapping the body with entutils.TransactingRepo — this bypasses the ctx-bound transaction.
Embedding type-conversion logic inside CRUD methods instead of mapping.go.
Querying add-ons without WithRatecards eager load — callers expect rate cards always populated.
Hard-deleting rows instead of setting DeletedAt for soft delete.
Building raw SQL predicates for status filtering instead of using the established EffectiveFrom/EffectiveTo Ent predicates.

decisions.decision: TransactingRepo wraps every adapter method rather than passing *entdb.Tx explicitly.
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions are carried implicitly in ctx; explicit tx parameters leak plumbing to every call site and make helpers harder to compose.

decisions.decision: Rate card updates replace all existing cards (delete + bulk-create) rather than diffing.
decisions.rationale: Simplifies consistency guarantees — no partial state can exist after an update, and ordering/key uniqueness is enforced atomically.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new adapter method for an addon sub-resource

openmeter/productcatalog/addon/httpdrive.purpose: HTTP handler layer for the addon domain, adapting addon.Service to HTTP request/response using the generic httptransport.Handler pattern. Exposes a Handler interface consumed by the v1 Chi router.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs
patterns.description: Each operation returns a typed handler constructed with httptransport.NewHandler (no path params) or NewHandlerWithArgs (with path params). Three closures: decoder, operation, encoder.

patterns.name: Request/Response type aliases
patterns.description: Each handler declares type aliases for Request, Response, and Handler at the top of the function block, mapping to domain input/output types and api.* generated types.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved from context
patterns.description: Every decoder calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) via namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder.GetNamespace; failure returns 500 internal server error.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: ValidationErrorEncoder per resource kind
patterns.description: Every handler appends httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ValidationErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ResourceKindAddon)) so domain validation errors map to correct HTTP status codes.

patterns.name: Mapping in mapping.go only
patterns.description: All api<->domain conversions (FromAddon, AsCreateAddonRequest, AsUpdateAddonRequest) live in mapping.go. Handler closures call these helpers, never inline-convert.

patterns.name: Handler interface composed from sub-interfaces
patterns.description: Handler interface in driver.go embeds AddonHandler which lists all operation handler methods. Compile-time assertion var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) ensures implementation.

key_file_guides.role: Handler + AddonHandler interface definition, handler struct, New constructor, resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New operations must be added to both the AddonHandler interface and the handler struct. resolveNamespace must not be bypassed.

key_file_guides.role: One function per HTTP operation (ListAddons, CreateAddon, UpdateAddon, DeleteAddon, GetAddon, PublishAddon, ArchiveAddon), each returning its typed handler.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues=true is set on create/update requests — preserve this for non-breaking validation. Delete returns http.StatusNoContent with EmptyResponseEncoder.

key_file_guides.role: api<->domain type conversion: FromAddon (domain->api), AsCreateAddonRequest / AsUpdateAddonRequest (api->domain). Currency validation happens here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Currency.Validate() must be called after assignment. RateCards mapped via productcatalog/http package helpers (http.AsRateCards, http.FromRateCard).

anti_patterns: Calling domain services directly in decoder functions — decoders must only parse/validate HTTP input and return a domain input type.
Inline type conversion in handler closures instead of using mapping.go helpers.
Omitting WithErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ValidationErrorEncoder(...)) — validation errors will not map to 400.
Adding new handler methods to handler struct without adding them to the Handler/AddonHandler interface.
Using context.Background() inside handler closures instead of the ctx passed to the operation closure.

decisions.decision: Handler methods return typed handler values rather than implementing http.Handler directly.
decisions.rationale: The httptransport.Handler[Req,Resp] pattern separates decoding, operation, and encoding, making each independently testable and composable with middleware via Chain.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new addon sub-resource HTTP operation

openmeter/productcatalog/addon/service.purpose: Business logic layer implementing addon.Service: versioned add-on lifecycle (create/update/delete/publish/archive), feature and tax code resolution for rate cards, and event publishing via Watermill.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wrapping for mutating operations
patterns.description: Create, Delete, Publish, Archive, and UpdateAddon (when rate cards change) wrap their fn in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, fn) to ensure atomicity across adapter calls + event publish.

patterns.name: resolveFeatures before persisting rate cards
patterns.description: CreateAddon and UpdateAddon call s.resolveFeatures(ctx, ns, &params.RateCards) to bi-directionally populate missing FeatureKey/FeatureID from the feature connector before saving.
patterns.example: if err = s.resolveFeatures(ctx, params.Namespace, &params.RateCards); err != nil { ... }

patterns.name: resolveTaxCodes before persisting rate cards
patterns.description: After resolveFeatures, s.resolveTaxCodes populates TaxConfig.TaxCodeID by calling taxCode.GetOrCreateByAppMapping for each rate card with a Stripe code.
patterns.example: if err = s.resolveTaxCodes(ctx, params.Namespace, &params.RateCards); err != nil { ... }

patterns.name: addonVersions collection for version management
patterns.description: getAddonVersions fetches all versions (including deleted) and returns addonVersions slice. HasDraft() guards against multiple concurrent draft versions. Latest() drives auto-increment of params.Version.

patterns.name: Event publishing after every mutation
patterns.description: Every mutating operation publishes a typed domain event (NewAddonCreateEvent, NewAddonDeleteEvent, etc.) via s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event) inside the transaction fn.
patterns.example: event := addon.NewAddonCreateEvent(ctx, aa); if err = s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event); err != nil { ... }

patterns.name: Config struct with validation in New constructor
patterns.description: service.New(Config) validates all required dependencies (Adapter, Feature, TaxCode, Logger, Publisher) before constructing the service. Returns addon.Service interface.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, New constructor, service struct definition. All dependencies injected at construction time.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All five dependencies are required — never make them optional without explicit noop fallbacks.

key_file_guides.role: All addon.Service method implementations. Contains resolveFeatures, resolveTaxCodes, addonVersions helper type, and getAddonVersions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolveFeatures converts GenericNotFoundError to GenericValidationError (feature not found = invalid input). Only draft add-ons can be updated; active/archived add-ons reject changes via ValidateAddonWithStatus.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests for full add-on lifecycle: create→publish→v2 create→publish→archive. Uses pctestutils.NewTestEnv.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests verify single-draft enforcement (HasDraft), version auto-increment, and status transition correctness.

key_file_guides.role: Dedicated tests for tax code dual-write (TaxConfig.TaxCodeID population) covering create/update/remove scenarios.
key_file_guides.watch_for: assertAddonRCDBCols directly queries the DB to verify dedicated tax_code_id and tax_behavior columns — use this pattern to verify low-level persistence in future tax-related changes.

anti_patterns: Calling s.adapter methods directly without transaction.Run for operations that combine multiple writes or event publishing.
Skipping resolveFeatures/resolveTaxCodes when accepting rate cards from callers — FeatureKey/FeatureID and TaxCodeID must be resolved before persisting.
Allowing multiple non-deleted draft versions for the same key — HasDraft() guard must be checked before creating.
Publishing domain events outside the transaction fn — event publish failure should roll back the DB write.
Using context.Background() instead of the passed ctx in service methods.

decisions.decision: Version auto-increment is computed from existing versions rather than relying on a DB sequence.
decisions.rationale: Enables multi-version branching (draft on top of active) with explicit version semantics visible in the domain model, and avoids coupling version numbering to DB identity.

decisions.decision: Tax code resolution (GetOrCreateByAppMapping) is performed at the service layer, not the adapter layer.
decisions.rationale: Tax code entity creation is a side effect that requires cross-domain coordination (taxcode.Service); the adapter layer must stay focused on Ent persistence of what it receives.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new addon mutating operation following the established pattern
